title: Name collision: 3 different IconProps interfaces in packages/radiants
summary: Three distinct interfaces all named IconProps coexist in the radiants package. packages/radiants/icons/types.ts defines an SVG component shape (extends SVGProps<SVGSVGElement>) consumed by generated.tsx, generated-24.tsx, DesktopIcons.tsx. packages/radiants/icons/Icon.tsx defines a dynamic-loader component shape (name, size, large, className, aria-label, basePath). packages/radiants/components/core/Icon/Icon.meta.ts declares a near-identical shape to the dynamic loader but is not linked to it. Icon.tsx imports the SVG shape under an alias (SvgIconProps), which proves both shapes are needed, but the same identifier IconProps is shadowed inside the file. This makes grep/rename/IDE refactors brittle and allows drift.
proposed_action: Rename Icon.tsx's local interface to DynamicIconProps (or IconLoaderProps) and export it from Icon.meta.ts. Have Icon.tsx import the type from ./Icon.meta (matches the Button/Meter pattern). Keep packages/radiants/icons/types.ts IconProps as the SVG component contract, unchanged.

title: Switch meta type drifted from runtime prop shape (missing 4 props)
summary: Switch.meta.ts declares SwitchProps { checked?, onChange?, size?, disabled?, label?, labelPosition?, id? } while Switch.tsx's SwitchProps additionally includes name, required, readOnly, and className. The meta-owned type is incomplete; the meta contract therefore silently misses fields that are part of the public API. The meta is authoritative for the registry but has already drifted.
proposed_action: Export the SwitchProps interface (and the SwitchSize alias) from Switch.meta.ts (add className/name/required/readOnly). Have Switch.tsx import type { SwitchProps, SwitchSize } from './Switch.meta'. Same pattern Button already uses via ./Button.meta.

title: Dialog/Popover/Combobox meta types declare callbacks as 'string'
summary: In DialogProps/PopoverProps (and to a lesser extent ComboboxProps), onOpenChange is typed as `string` in the meta-owned interface. This is almost certainly a copy-paste artifact from the authoring flow (description strings and types got conflated). The runtime Popover.tsx correctly types it as `(open: boolean, eventDetails?: unknown) => void`. Because defineComponentMeta<TProps>() uses TProps to generic-constrain variants/exampleProps, a wrong TProps weakens the generic.
proposed_action: Fix the meta interface to type onOpenChange as the actual callback signature. Simultaneously export the corrected type and import from the .tsx side to prevent re-drift (same consolidation pattern as TYPE-002).

title: Systematic <Component>Props duplication across .meta.ts and .tsx (radiants + ctrl)
summary: Approximately 25 component folders define a locally-scoped <Component>Props interface in both .meta.ts (to generic-constrain defineComponentMeta<TProps>()) and .tsx (for the runtime component signature). The shapes are near-identical for many of them (plus `children`/`className` on the tsx side) but there is no static link between them, so they drift independently (proven by TYPE-002/TYPE-003). Button, Meter, and Input already demonstrate the fix: either export the prop type from .meta.ts and import in .tsx (Button, Meter), or hoist shared fields into a .types.ts next to the .meta.ts (Input → Input.types.ts, InputShellProps/TextAreaShellProps).
proposed_action: For each listed component, pick the existing Button/Meter/Input model (export shared type from one sibling file; import in the other). Treat per-component as its own sub-finding for execution — exclusive write scope per component, low blast radius each. Start with components whose .meta and .tsx have verbatim shape matches (ChipTag, Card, Tooltip). Compound-component .tsx files that split a single .meta prop surface into multiple Root/Content/Trigger interfaces (Alert, Dialog, Combobox, Menubar, NavigationMenu) are lower priority and can keep their .tsx split while exporting only the public Root-level prop type.

title: Avatar enum aliases duplicated between .tsx CVA-derived type and .meta.ts inline strings
summary: Avatar.meta.ts authors `type AvatarSize = "sm"|"md"|"lg"|"xl"` and `type AvatarShape = "circle"|"square"` locally. Avatar.tsx derives the same shapes implicitly via VariantProps<typeof avatarVariants>. These can be unified by exporting AvatarSize/AvatarShape from Avatar.meta.ts and using them in both the CVA variant keys and the interface fields (same pattern Button uses with ButtonMode/ButtonTone/ButtonSize/ButtonRounded).
proposed_action: Export AvatarSize and AvatarShape from Avatar.meta.ts. Reference them in Avatar.tsx (either via VariantProps or by typing the cva variants explicitly with these aliases).

title: Card enum aliases duplicated between .tsx and .meta.ts
summary: Card.tsx authors `type CardVariant = 'default'|'inverted'|'raised'` and `type CardRounded = 'sm'|'md'|'lg'`. Card.meta.ts inlines the same enum strings in its CardProps interface and in props.variant.values / props.rounded.values. Card.tsx builds a CARD_PIXEL_CLASS record keyed by CardRounded — the alias already exists on the tsx side, it just isn't shared.
proposed_action: Move CardVariant and CardRounded into Card.meta.ts, export them, and import in Card.tsx. Card.meta.ts's CardProps becomes { variant?: CardVariant; rounded?: CardRounded } referring to those exports.

title: Alert variant enum duplicated between .tsx and .meta.ts
summary: Alert.tsx authors `type AlertVariant = 'default'|'success'|'warning'|'error'|'info'` and uses it in AlertRootProps. Alert.meta.ts redefines the same enum via an inline union inside AlertProps, and in props.variant.values. Alert.tsx's AlertVariant also keys the VARIANT_ICONS record. The .meta.ts is authoritative per the dispatch rule — export AlertVariant from Alert.meta.ts and have Alert.tsx import it.
proposed_action: Move AlertVariant into Alert.meta.ts as an exported type alias. Update Alert.tsx to `import type { AlertVariant } from './Alert.meta'`. AlertRootProps, AlertChildProps, AlertCloseProps remain in .tsx (they are compound-subcomponent-only, not the root meta contract).

title: ChipTag prop interface duplicated verbatim between .meta.ts and .tsx
summary: ChipTag.meta.ts and ChipTag.tsx independently declare near-identical ChipTagProps interfaces. The only difference is that ChipTag.tsx uses `size?: ControlSize` (imported from packages/ctrl/primitives/types.ts) while ChipTag.meta.ts inlines `size?: 'sm'|'md'|'lg'`. This is a prime same-shape consolidation target and also an interesting sanity-check case: if the .meta.ts imports ControlSize from primitives/types.ts, the meta type becomes the single source of truth without pulling in runtime-only concerns.
proposed_action: Export ChipTagProps from ChipTag.meta.ts with `size?: ControlSize` (importing ControlSize from '../../primitives/types'). Update ChipTag.tsx to `import type { ChipTagProps } from './ChipTag.meta'`. Delete the duplicate interface in ChipTag.tsx.

title: Redundant 'sm'|'md'|'lg' size aliases across Switch/Input/ctrl primitives
summary: Three packages each define an identical `'sm'|'md'|'lg'` type alias (SwitchSize, InputSize, ControlSize). These are not the same concept semantically (different components can evolve separately), so consolidation would be a premature abstraction. Report-only. If a future migration unifies size language across the system, ControlSize in @rdna/ctrl is the natural canonical name — but radiants cannot depend on ctrl, so any such move would require hoisting into a new shared sub-package. Not recommended now.
proposed_action: No action. Report-only. Keep each alias local to its owning component family.

title: Pretext primitive settings types — verify no per-primitive redefinition
summary: EditorialSettings/BroadsheetSettings/BookSettings are authored in apps/rad-os/components/apps/pretext/types.ts. Per the global guardrail, pretext authoring primitives are referenced by name from generators/runtime loaders and should not be consolidated away. Reported because each primitive has its own *-layout.ts file that may re-describe the same fields inline; no evidence found in this audit, but manual verification recommended before any Stage 2 work touches pretext.
proposed_action: No action beyond verification read of the three primitive layout files to confirm they consume the exported interfaces rather than re-declare them.
